entry.title: Kickstart Your Developer Journey – Get SAP-Certified in Low-Code/No-Code
entry.content:
New to development? No problem! SAP offers a certification and learning resources that help you build real business apps, workflows, and user experiences — without needing to be a coding expert. Using tools like SAP Build Apps, Work Zone, and Process Automation, you’ll be surprised how much you can do just by dragging, dropping, and configuring.

 

Simple Steps to Get Certified

 

Step 1: Learn at Your Own Pace Follow these beginner-friendly learning paths:

  • SAP Build Apps: Build apps visually without needing to write code. Learn how to connect data, create user-friendly pages, and define app behavior.
  • SAP Build Work Zone: Set up a digital workspace where users can access their apps and content in one place.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: Learn how to build simple workflows and automation tools to save time and reduce manual work.

Step 2: Practice What You Learn You’ll try a real-world simulation using everything you’ve learned. This hands-on practice is required before taking the certification exam and helps boost your confidence.

Step 3: Take the Certification Exam Once you feel ready, book your exam. Passing it proves your skills and gives you an official SAP Certification — a great addition to your resume!

 

Want to Keep Growing?

 

  • Watch tutorials on YouTube from SAP Developer Advocates.
  • Join local SAP CodeJam events to meet and learn from other developers.
  • Subscribe to SAP Learning Hub for more guided learning from SAP experts.

 

Whether you're starting your career or switching to tech, this certification is a great way to get hands-on with real business tools — and build your future.

entry.title: Make use of Generative AI to accelerate development in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automation is SAP’s centralized development environment for intelligent automation and process extensibility. It has already been implemented by hundreds of customers to increase overall process efficiency and free employees from mundane and repetitive tasks.

A key driver of its success is the ease of use of the development environment and the low entry barrier for different stakeholders who want to participate in process development projects. This is largely due to an intuitive graphical layout designer, a simplified commercial model that allows you to start small and fast, and pre-built automation content available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Now, process automation becomes even simpler with the native integration of generative AI within the SAP Build Process Automation environment.

You can use generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation to generate business processes, decisions, forms, and script tasks. The goal of this blog post is to guide you on your journey to explore this feature and share some best practices.

 

System setup

To use the generative AI features, you must first enable generative AI at the tenant level and accept the terms and conditions, by navigating to the Tenant Configuration within the Control Tower:

 

 

Once enabled, the Generate function will become available after approximately 5 minutes, both in the Overview screen of the project and within the process editor:

 

 

Use-Case

In the next section, we’ll explore the key capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation that leverage Generative AI. To provide meaningful context, we’ll walk through a real-world scenario: imagine you're a senior automation developer aiming to create new process artifacts - such as Business Rules, Conditions, Process Flows, and Approval Steps. Your goal is to use natural language prompts to generate these components efficiently. We’ll demonstrate how Generative AI can accelerate this process and show you how to craft effective prompts to get the results you want. Below are some practical examples for following artifacts:

 

  • Generate a Data Type
  • Generate a Process
  • Generate a Form
  • Generate a Decision
  • Generate a Script
  • Summarize the Process

 

Generate a Data Type

We begin our automation journey by defining a Data Type. This is a crucial step, as it later enables us to handle input and output values in Conditions and Business Rules. To get started, navigate to the process overview page of your project and click on Generate:

 

 

Next, select Data Type and enter your prompt. Below are two example prompts you can use and refine as needed:

  • "Generate a data type to handle Purchasing Organizations. This data type should contain a single field named purchasingOrganizations, represented as a string."

  • "Generate a data type to handle Approvers. This data type should include two fields: approversEmail and approversName, both represented as strings."

These prompts can be adjusted iteratively to match your specific needs. As a result you now have two different Data Types which you can use as input and output values for your process steps:

 

 

Generate a Process

The next step is to generate a process flow that brings together multiple artifacts - such as Data Types, Decisions, Conditions, Forms, and more. As in the previous step, navigate to the overview page of your project and select Process. Here, you can enter a prompt to define your process logic. Below is an example you can use as a starting point:

  • "Generate a new process. Begin with a business rule to determine the approver. Then, add a condition to handle cost thresholds: if the cost exceeds 2,000 euros, trigger an approval step; if the cost is below 2,000 euros, trigger an action project to post the purchase requisition to SAP S/4HANA."

Feel free to iterate on this prompt or expand it to fit your specific process requirements.
As a result, you now have a generated process that already includes several key artifacts. From here, you can begin refining the process further or add additional components—such as user tasks, forms, notifications, or integrations - as needed to align it with your business logic and automation goals:

 

 

Generate a Form

We’ve noticed that the current process is missing a trigger—a key component to initiate the workflow. Fortunately, we can use Generative AI to create this for us. In this context, Generative AI can help generate various types of forms, including trigger forms, notification forms, and approval forms.

There’s no need to return to the overview page—you can stay within the Process Builder. Simply click on the Generate button and enter your prompt. Here’s an example:

  • "Add a trigger form to start the process. In this form, the user should enter product information, quantity, and price."

This form will serve as the entry point for the process, capturing the necessary input to drive subsequent steps.

 

 

Sometimes, the generated form may appear in an unexpected location within the process flow. Don’t worry—this is easy to fix. Simply delete the form from its current position in the flow.

To correctly assign it as the trigger:

  1. Click on Add Trigger

  2. Select Forms

  3. Choose the newly created form from the list

This will properly link the form as the starting point of your process, as you can see here:

 

 

As a post processing step I change the input text for the line-items with a table list to enter multiple values.

 

Generate a Decision

Next, we’ll incorporate a Decision into the automation flow. Decisions can be used for anything, but in our scenario, we’ll use a decision table to determine the appropriate approver based on the purchasing group.

To add a Decision:

  1. Click the plus (+) button at the point in the flow where you want to insert the artifact.

  2. Select Decision.

  3. Configure the decision by defining proper input and output values.

You can select from the Data Types we previously created to ensure your decision logic is well-integrated and functional:

 

 

To define the logic for your Decision, go to the Rules tab and click on Generate to enter your prompt. This is where you can use Generative AI to quickly create a decision table. Here’s an example prompt you can use:

  • "Create a decision table rule named 'Approver Determination' to assign approvers based on the purchasing organization. If the purchasing organization is 1710, the purchase requisition must be approved by Admin Manager 1 (manager1@sap.com). If the purchasing organization is 1720, it must be approved by Admin Manager 2 (manager2@sap.com)."

This rule will automatically evaluate input from the process and route it to the correct approver accordingly. Here you can see the final result:

 

 

Generate a Script

SAP Build Process Automation also supports the use of custom scripts within a process flow. These scripts are particularly useful for tasks such as data validation, calculations, or entity mapping. In this example, we’ll use a custom script to calculate the total price from an input table.

To do this:

  1. Click the plus (+) button at the point in the process where you want the script to run.

  2. Select Script from the available options.

  3. Click Generate and enter the following prompt:

  • "Add a custom script to sum all prices from the table list and calculate a total price."

This will generate a script step that performs the calculation and makes the result available for downstream steps in the process.

 

Once it is finished I can either accept or reject the proposed script. I can also amend the script which is necessary in some cases:

 

 

Summarize the process

In addition to the artifacts generated by the new GenAI feature, I’ve further refined the process—mapping the input and output values, and making additional adjustments to align with my specific requirements. As a result, we now have a completed and deployable process automation, ready to be made available to business users:

 

 

To ensure proper documentation for stakeholders like process owners, you can easily generate a summary of your entire process flow. Simply click the Generate button within the Process Builder, then select Explain this process. This will provide a clear, detailed overview of your process, making it easier for others to understand and review:

 

 

This will generate a detailed written documentation that you can easily share or upload to internal platforms such as your Wiki, or tools like Signavio or LeanIX. This ensures that the process is well-documented and accessible to all relevant stakeholders.

 

 

 

Some Tips and Tricks

When crafting a prompt, it’s important to be as precise as possible. Always use the verbs "Generate" or "Add", and reference the specific SAP Build Process Automation artifact names. Your prompt description should be between 5 and 1,000 characters in length.

If you're not satisfied with the result, you can either delete the artifact and generate a new one, or use the graphical layout designer to modify the artifact as needed. Additionally, beyond process steps, you can define process variables such as inputs, outputs, and custom variables to further refine your process.

If you run the generation to update an existing process, the following restrictions apply:

  • The process variables (input, output, custom) are not updated or removed.
  • Object-type inputs that refer to a data type are not supported.

Also make sure that you get familiar with all the known limitations on this feature, which are documented in our Help-Portal.

 

Happy hacking!

entry.title: SAP Document AI — The Roadmap for Seamless Document Processing in SAP Business Suite
entry.content:
In today's digital world, efficient document processing has become an essential aspect of business operations. SAP Document AI is at the forefront of this transformation, providing innovative solutions that enhance productivity and streamline processes. Read more about the recent announcement here.

Upcoming Excellence with SAP Document AI Premium Plus 

Looking ahead, the SAP Document AI promises to simplify business document processing throughout SAP Business Suite through new, diverse and value-adding integration scenarios. For this purpose we plan to introduce the Premium Plus edition later this year. This edition harnesses the power of the latest generative AI technology, provides multi language support, offers turn-key integrations for enriched processing capabilities, instant learning capabilities, and harmonized experience across SAP’s portfolio.

 As part of the upcoming 2508 release of SAP Cloud ERP Public, the Premium Plus capabilities will enhance document efficiency with following embedded scenarios:

  • Quality Management: Automate the processing of incoming Supplier Quality Certificates to ensure quality compliance quickly and accurately.
  • Order Management: Expedite sales operations with automatic extraction of sales orders from documents in a variety of languages.
  • Inventory Management: Streamline inventory management by extracting key data from delivery notes for seamless tracking.

Users will benefit from end-to-end document processing automation, beginning with document ingestion, through AI processing, and culminating in matching with corresponding business objects within SAP Cloud ERP. All scenarios are also planned to be ported to the private edition soon [1].

For a hands-on look, watch the demo video depicting the processing of incoming supplier quality certificates in Quality Management:

Outlook and Roadmap for the entire SAP Business Suite

SAP’s vision for document processing integrates seamlessly into the current landscape and extends into the future. Future integrations of SAP Document AI include:

  • SAP SuccessFactors: Pre-configured content for high-volume HR documents like "National ID validation during New Hire Onboarding."
  • SAP Fieldglass: Efficiently upload and process Statements of Work within Services Procurement, simplifying document extraction and drafting.
  • SAP Enterprise Service Management: Specialized Document AI capabilities for business documents posting, including payment advice, into S/4HANA.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: Access to Generative AI capabilities for template-free document automation.
  • SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management: Enhanced invoice data extraction capabilities for superior invoice capture.

SAP Document AI continues to redefine document processing, offering businesses enhanced capabilities that drive efficiency, reduce errors, and empower organizations to focus on strategic tasks. Stay tuned and join our community of users on SAP Community. Stay updated on upcoming AI use case releases by exploring the SAP Roadmap Explorer. Explore more about SAP Document AI by visiting http://www.sap.com/ai/document-ai and don’t miss out on SAP's latest AI innovations at Sapphire 2025.

 

Author's note:

1. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition users can already leverage SAP Document AI capabilities featuring Generative AI, notably by subscribing to and integrating with SAP Document AI Premium Edition via SAP BTP.

2. SAP Document AI, previously known as Document Information Extraction, has been available in variety of integrative scenarios across SAP as a "base" capability e.g. SAP Concur Invoice Management, SAP Cash Application, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management and many more – check out this blog post for more details.

entry.title: Empowering business to manage Decisions in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automation is a unified, low-code solution designed to help customers to digitize and streamline business operations. A core component of this offering is Decisions. Decision artefacts helps to decouple complex rules from your code, making them easier to maintain and evolve over a period of time. Whether you are implementing approval policies, pricing logic, eligibility checks, or compliance controls, business rules can be defined in a structured, transparent format. These rules are integrated seamlessly into processes, ensuring consistency and flexibility in end to end processes.

🚀 Today, we're excited to launch a dedicated application (Studio Home) that empowers business users to view and/or maintain decisions without needing to navigate into SAP Build Lobby, rather from a SAP Build Work Zone site.

Motivation

Decisions are created as part of a process automation project from the SAP Build Lobby. Lobby is a powerful application development environment designed for developers (and citizen developers). It support tasks like creating a new project, new data type, new decisions, new process, automation, etc. However, it is not necessarily optimized for an everyday business users who often own the logic behind the business rules (Decisions in SAP Build Process Automation).

Figure 1 shows a project as seen in the Lobby. In this example, the Lobby project contains additional artefact like Data Type, information like Creation, Bundle Size, Collaborators, Project Settings, Triggers, Dependencies and actions like Create, Import, Release etc. While this is valuable for developers and citizen developers, it's overwhelming for business users whose primary need is to view or update data in existing decisions like maintain approval limits, routing logic, or adjusting discount rates. For these users, exposing unrelated artefacts and technical options adds unnecessary complexity. It also introduces a risk - users might unintentionally view or change artefacts or properties beyond their scope which can impact the stability of the overall project.

Hence until now, business users had no choice but to rely on developers to make even simple rule changes. This dependency created friction, delayed updates, and slowed down the entire change management cycle.

Figure 1: Project overview page as seen in the Lobby

Lightweight decision management for end users

Once configured by the SAP Build Workzone site administrator, the users are presented with application tiles in their SAP Build Workzone site. This respects all the roles, groups, space & catalog configuration of SAP Build Workzone. Each tile corresponds to one process automation project in the Lobby. The user can click on the tile as shown in the Figure 2, to navigate into the application to manage Decisions.

Figure 2: SAP Build Workzone application to manage decisions

They are presented with an application (Studio Home) that lists all the Decisions contained within a project for which the tile was configured. In this example project 'Purchase Requisition Decisions', there are three Decisions artefacts as represented in Figure 3.

Studio Home application

Figure 3: Studio Home application to manage decisions within a project

The Studio Home application is a simplified view of the Lobby project and shows only relevant & simplified information to the end user:

  1. Decision-Focused View: Only the relevant artefacts within the project (Decisions) are visible, keeping the interface clean and user-friendly.
  2. One-Click Deployment: A single button that lets users to automatically release a patch version of the project & deploy it by retaining all previous environment variables. The end users don't have to understand about patch, minor, major versioning, its implications, the concept of environments & environment variables.
  3. Design Console: Design Console that shows any error or warning messages during the release and deployment, similar to the experience in the Lobby project.
  4. Version History Access: Option to navigate and view the previous versions of the decision artefact in the project.

The user can select one Decision and navigate into the decision editor, that opens up a simplified decision editor. The user is presented with an interface that lists all the available rules (Decision Table and Text Rule) as shown in Figure 4. Unlike in Lobby, the user does not have options like inputs, outputs, variables of the Decision, nor they will have option to reorder/create new rule (decision table or text rule). The breadcrumbs control (highlighted in Figure 4) at the top of screen can be used to navigate back to the project. 

Figure 4: Decision editor with list of rules

When the user selects a rule (text rule or decision table), the appropriate rule editor is presented. The Figure 5 shows a decision table editor. The goal of this interface is also to be simple and only show what is relevant for a business users - edit the content of the decision table, export to excel, import from excel and save the changes. The decision table settings to define condition columns, result columns, setting the hit policy, etc. are not shown to the user. The similar is the experience for a text rule editor. Once the changes to the rules are made, the user can use the breadcrumb control at the top of screen to navigate to the project and deploy the changes with a single click of a button.

Figure 5: Decision table editor 

What else is new

We are constantly trying to improve the user experience for the end users and one area of our focus is expression editing in decision tables. Figure 6 shows the current state of expression editing in decision table. The expression editors in our rules had an auto-suggest list (3) which can be brought up by pressing the keys <ctrl> and <space> in the expression editor (2). To bring up the expression editor in a decision table, the decision table cell need to be clicked (1). Our customers shared a feedback that working with complex expressions is tedious with the auto-suggest and at the same time expert users find it less productive requiring a lot of clicks.

Figure 6: Expression editing in decision tables until today

While we are looking at making the expression editing more useable (watch this space for a redesigned expression editing), we have now enabled a small change in the decision table editor:

  • Select the cell (single-click) to start typing directly as shown in Figure 7. 
  • Double-click the cell to open the expression editor, especially for long/complex expressions.
  • Use <ctrl> and <space> (option + space for MacOS) anytime to bring up the auto-suggest list.

This makes simple edits faster and more intuitive while still supporting advanced expression editing. Syntax errors, if any, are automatically detected and highlighted.

Figure 7: Edit the expression directly in the cell with a single-click

How to set it up

Refer to the official help documentation for configuring a tile.

Tip:

On click on the "Deploy All" button the entire project is released as a version and that version is deployed. If there are other artefacts (like data type, automation, process) in the same project they are also included in the version deployment. There are possibilities of other artefacts in an error state, which prevents the deployment. If you would not want that behaviour, then move the decisions into a separate project. I will soon share in another blog post how one can setup a project like that.

Things to note

  • The Studio Home application can auto only be configured to deploy in a shared environment. If you are still using the Public environment to deploy the projects, refer to the blog from @Alex-Fortin about how to transition from public environment.
  • Since the user will still work on the project, release a version and deploy the user must have ProcessAutomationDeveloper role (advanced user).
  • The user should also have the authotization for the Lobby project.

Closing thoughts

The introduction of the new Studio Home for maintaining decisions marks an important step towards empowering business users to take ownership of maintaining and owning the data maintained in the Decisions artefact. By simplifying the way decisions are maintained we aim to reduce dependencies & accelerate change management process. Last but not the lease, this is just a beginning and we look forward to support intuitive and business-friendly decision management in SAP Build.

entry.title: Configure a tile on SAP Build Work Zone to manage Decision artifacts
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post, you will learn how to configure a tile on SAP Build Work Zone to manage decision artifact within a business process project in the new Studio Home application. You can use this application to access business process projects outside of the lobby and allow business users to manage specific decision in the project without viewing or affecting the other project artifacts.

Empowering business to manage Decisions in SAP Build Process Automation.

You need to create a tile on SAP Build Work Zone for every shared environment where you have deployed your business process projects.

Prerequisites

  • You have created at least one Decision artifact in a business process project. For more information, see  Create a Decision .
  • You would need to access the decision and should have the Process Automation Developer roles
  • You also have the edit or view authorization for that specific project

Procedure

  • Step 1:  Create a Service Instance

You have to create a new service instance for SAP Build Process Automation. The creation of the new service instance will have the app-host-ids of the studio home and decision applications. It should have more than 8 app-host-ids. For more information, see Create a Service Instance .  

  • Step 2: Configure SAP Build Process Automation Destinations.

You have to create a new destination for the service instance that contains the app_host_Id for the application. For more information, see Configure SAP Build Process Automation Destinations

Note: Ensure that you do not have any two destinations in a subaccount with sap.cloud.service = com.sap.spa.processautomation or sap.cloud.service.alias = spa properties. 

  • Step 3: Refresh the channel manager

    • Open the SAP Build Work Zone Admin Console.
    • Navigate to Channel Manager tab to the left and press the refresh button available on the page. The refresh button is selected to update the list of HTML5 Apps in the Content Manager. Once the refresh button is pressed you will be able to view the Studio Home app in the Content Manager.

  • Step 4: Create a Local Copy of Studio Home

Step 4.1: Navigate to Content Manager tab and click on the Content Explorer button to go to the Content Explorer  page which will have the Html5 Apps tile.

Step 4.2: Click on the Html5 apps tile which will open the list of Html5 Apps. Search for the Studio Home App and Select and click Add button to add the application to your subaccount. If you do not see the Studio Home application refer to the troubleshooting.

Step 4.3: Open the Studio Home application in the Content Manager choose Create a local copy

Step 4.4: Choose Edit and adapt the texts in the General section. You can use a custom title and description for the tile.

Note: optionally you can adapt translation by navigating to the Translation Tab.

Step 4.5: In the Parameters section, enter the following values:

Name
Default Value
Id

Enter the project identifier.

Note: You can find the project identifier on the General tab in the Project Properties in the Lobby. In the business process project choose

 

 Manage the project properties.

modesaas
titleEnter a title for your tile
bpi.decision{"mode":"basic")
artifactTypesbpi.decision
environmentId

Enter the value of the Identifier.

Note:You can find the value of the identifier in the Lobby by opening the required environment from the list under the Control Tower > Environments tile.

UseUrlFullNamespacetrue

Step 4.6: Choose Save.

  • Step 5:Add the local copy of the Studio Home app to a group, catalog, or role

Add the local copy of the Studio Home app to a group, catalog, or role and make sure they are visible to users. You can create a role and assign to the user who can have access to this Studio Home app. You can add this application to the default Everyone role for all the users to access the Studio Home application. For more information, see Assign Apps to a Group and to a Catalog and Assign Content to a Role.

  • Step 6: Create and Navigate to a site in the SAP Build Work Zone Admin Console

Create a site if is not available for the user else you can use the existing site and assign the role which you created in the previous step for the Studio Home Application, by default Everyone role is selected and If you add the Studio Home Application to it, the application would be visible to everyone.

Result

Open the site and you will be able to the see the tile created for the Studio Home application under the group you had created.

 

Troubleshooting

The Studio Home App is not available in the SAP Build Work Zone.

If you do not see the Studio Home application in the SAP Build Work Zone's Content Manager, try the following troubleshooting steps:

Ensure that:

  • the service Key you created for the new SAP Build service instance has more than 8 app-host-IDs. Refer step1 for creating a new service instance.
  • you do not have more than one destination with the following properties:
    • sap.cloud.service = com.sap.spa.processautomation and
    • sap.cloud.service.alias = spa.

Refer step2 for creating a new Destination.

Error in Deployment

If the deployment of the project fails when you choose Deploy All without making any changes to the project, try the following troubleshooting steps:

  • The Deploy All feature deploys the entire project, including artifacts other than Decisions. The IT/ dev needs to make sure that the project is error-free so that the business users can maintain the decisions. If the project is failing with errors to the artifact other than decisions you can check the project in the Lobby . 
  • If the decision artifact is invalid, go to the invalid decision and check the console for any error that occured while maintaining the decisions. Once you resolve all the errors in the console, you can redeploy by choosing Deploy All.

Summary

You successfully created a tile for the Studio Home application to maintain the Decisions created without the use of Lobby. Using the tile you can also create a role and assign it to only specific users who can access the decision.

entry.title: New SAP Architecture Center: Foundation for Enterprise Architecture & Architects
entry.content:
The new Introduction from SAP, the  SAP Architecture Center is among one of the best addition to SAP's resources, making a centralized hub for SAP solution reference architectures. Through this addition, businesses can adopt SAP Solutions in a more effective manner, extracting valuable business insights from organizational data.

 

Key Features of the SAP Architecture Center

  • Comprehensive Reference Architectures: The Architecture Center provides a wide range of reference solution architectures, covering multiple business needs though technological domains and design patterns.
  • Technology Domains: It caters various domains which include Automation, Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Enterprise Integration, Governance and Security.
  • Technology Partners: SAP Architecture Center includes architecture involving major technology players like GCP, Microsoft Azure, AWS etc.
  • Interactive Exploration Tools: SAP Architecture Center users will be able to navigate the architecture through using tools like Overview, Architecture Explorer, Card-Style, and Navigator Style Overview
  • Community Engagement: For community interaction, there is a dedicated section, encouraging collaboration and knowledge sharing among the SAP professionals.

 

SAP Viewpoints

 

Architecture Explorer:

Through the architecture explorer you navigate through the Technology Domains like integrations, AI, App Development & more and Technology Partners like AWS, Azure and GCP, discovering solution blueprints based on your specific requirement like you are  planning an AI Driven use case on Azure or exploring data Architecture AWS, the Architecture Center guides through the most inline architecture assets with ease.

 

Technology Domains:

  • Application Development & Automation: Build and extend SAP applications using low-code tools, CAP, and event-driven architectures.

  • Artificial Intelligence: Integrate AI and machine learning into business processes with SAP AI Core and Launchpad.

  • Data & Analytics: Design unified data strategies with SAP Datasphere, BW/4HANA, and Analytics Cloud.

  • Integration: Enable secure and scalable integration across cloud and on-premise systems using SAP Integration Suite.

  • Operations & Security: Implement best practices for system security, observability, DevOps, and compliance.

 

Technology Domains:

The SAP Architecture Center focuses on the reference architectures across leading hyperscalers

  • Microsoft Azure
  • AWS
  • GCP

Even if you are using GCP for orchestrating analytics workloads, running BTP services on Azure or data Pipelines on AWS. The SAP Architecture center provides architectures patterns for each environment.

 

Reference Architectures:

SAP Architecture Center contains a rich repository of reference Architectures aligned with the recommended practices, well structured and reusable blueprints that elaborates how SAP Solutions are developed, integrated, deployed and scaled real world Enterprise Scenarios. Reference Architectures within the SAP Architecture Center is formulated in key sections, enriching the overall architecture in a more organized and easy to understand way. 


Conclusion:

Looking ahead, SAP Architecture Center will play a vital role driving standardized and future scoped solutions. As all the technologies are going through a continuous evolving process and towards a more modular, cloud native and AI Integrated solutions, and SAP heading towards the same,  many more things would be part of the SAP Architecture Center in the coming days which will accelerate digital transformations, ensuring architectural consistency. By adopting these reference architectures, your organization can future proof their Digital Transformation and IT Strategy with reduced complexity.

entry.title: Understanding "My Processes" and "My Requests" in SBPA: Empowering Users Through Clarity and Control
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automation is a low-code/no-code platform by SAP that enables businesses to automate workflows and business processes quickly and with minimal programming effort. 

As organizations increasingly adopt automation to streamline business workflows, user-centric features become essential for both transparency and efficiency. Two key views that support this in SAP Build Process Automation are: 
“My Processes” 
“My Requests” 

While they may sound similar, they serve distinct purposes in helping users and teams monitor and interact with automated processes. Let’s explore what they are, why they matter, and how they enhance the overall automation experience. 

 

Problem Statement: 

As organizations adopt workflow automation to streamline operations, users often struggle to track and manage their interactions with automated systems. Despite automation improving execution speed and consistency, lack of transparency and user feedback remains a major issue. 

 

The Problem: 

Users currently lack intuitive, centralized views within SAP Build Process Automation that clearly separate and present (a) the complex end-to-end business processes they participate in and (b) their individual service or task requests—leading to confusion, inefficient follow-ups, and reduced trust in automation. 

This results in: 

  • Uncertainty about the current status of submitted requests (e.g., approvals, service tickets, form submissions) 
  • Inability to monitor progress or identify delays in multi-step business processes 
  • Increased support queries to help desks or process owners asking for updates 
  • Low adoption or engagement with self-service automation tools due to poor visibility 

 

Why It Matters: 

  • Business users need a “My Requests” view to track simple, individual submissions quickly. 
  • Power users or process participants need a “My Processes” view to monitor end-to-end workflows they’re involved in or responsible for. 

 

The Need: 

There is a clear need for two distinct, yet complementary, views: 

  • “My Requests” – to provide users with a simple interface for tracking their personal submissions and their current statuses. 
  • “My Processes” – to allow users to oversee, participate in, and manage the lifecycle of complex, automated workflows. 

 

What are "My Processes" Views? 

“My Processes” application is dedicated to Process Participants like Approvers for example. This application would help the Process Participants easily track, understand, and make informed decisions about where their input is needed and how it fits into the larger process.   

 

Key Use Cases: 

  • Tracking the status of an employee onboarding process 
  • Monitoring a multi-level purchase approval 
  • Managing a customer service workflow 

 

Benefits: 

  • Real-time insight into the process lifecycle 
  • Accountability by showing who is responsible for each task 
  • Faster issue resolution when processes stall or bottleneck 
  • Ideal for process owners, managers, and workflow participants 

 

What are "My Requests" Views? 

"My Requests” View provides clarity to the Requestors on how their request fits into the workflow, the status of their request, and what actions (if any) they need to take as their request moves through the approval or fulfilment process.   

 

Key Use Cases: 

  • Viewing a submitted IT support ticket 
  • Checking status of a travel reimbursement 
  • Tracking a leave request form 

 

Benefits: 

  • Self-service tracking—no need to email or call for updates 
  • Simple, clean interface focused on the user's submissions 
  • Encourages user engagement with automation without needing to understand complex workflows 

 

Key Differences 

Feature 

My Processes 

My Requests 

Focus 

Entire process lifecycle 

Individual request status 

User Role 

Participant or process owner 

Requester or submitter 

Complexity 

Often involves multiple steps 

Usually single or few steps 

 

Prerequisites 

Here are a few Prerequisites for My Processes and My Requests View. 

 

Let’s explore the configuration details:

Configure My Processes Application 

Configure My Requests 

Now that both My Requests and My Processes tiles have been configured, let’s look at how we can visualise them: 

 

Entry Screen: 

 

My Requests view provides an overview of at all the requests initiated by you based on the filter criteria. You also have the possibility to look at the details of each request individually, the stage at which the request currently is in, the history of the previous steps, the status of the steps and the details of the approvers. You also have the possibility to look at all the tasks under a sub-process step (even if there is a hierarchy) and gain insights, without moving away from the lead process and much more. 

 My Requests

 

My Processes application provides an overview of all the processes that you have been given access to. You can also have a look at details of individual process instances and have the same capabilities as in “My Requests” view.  

 My Processes

Additional Key features are listed here. 

 

Conclusion 

As more businesses embrace automation, features like “My Processes” and “My Requests” ensure that automation remains accessible, visible, and actionable. By making it easier to track, monitor, and engage with automated processes, SAP Build Process Automation delivers not just technical power—but real business value. 

 

More Information: 

Configure My Processes Application 

Configure My Requests 

Use My Processes Application 

SAP BTP Innboytes blog post

This is only the beginning - stay tuned!

entry.title: Mastering Complex SBPA Approvals : A Deep Dive into Wait Steps, Script Tasks, and API Integrations
entry.content:
Welcome back to our new blog series,  our previous blog series  delved into the intricacies of dynamic sequential approval workflows.

In this two-part series, my colleague @Adwait_Fadnavis  and I will explore some very critical business workflow  requirements  in detail using SAP Build Process Automation. 

Throughout this blog series, we will delve into the following critical business requirements:

Part 1 - Current Blog :Mastering Complex SBPA Approvals : A Deep Dive into Wait Steps, Script Tasks, and API Integrations

Part 2 : Demonstration with Complete end to end testing 

We will demonstrate how to use various SAP Build Process Automation tools to solve real-time challenges effectively.

This blog post addresses this complex requirement with detailed step-by-step instructions. You'll learn how to ensure workflows advance smoothly, even with  approval condition utilizing various innovative latest features such as: Wait Step with API Triggering , Workflow Context update via API etc.

Business Context and Problem Statement

While working on multiple SAP Build Process Automation projects, we have faced situations where workflows only proceed if all departments or approvers approve them unanimously.

If any approver rejects the proposal, the entire workflow is rejected . This creates a need for a parallel approval process, adhering to an "all-or-nothing" requirement, which SAP's standard solutions do not support.

In a below sample procurement workflow, the request is created and submitted. Both the Department Manager and Finance Department must approve it simultaneously. If either rejects, the workflow is terminated.

If both approve, the request goes to the Executive for final approval. The Executive's decision determines whether the request is processed or terminated.

Dynamic Number of Parallel Approval
Technical Solutioning & Design

In this process, we will create two workflows using SAP Build Process Automation’s Low Code/No Code approach.

Let’s refer to these as Workflow A (main workflow) and Workflow B.

Workflow A( Main Workflow): This is the main workflow, triggered with all input parameters, responsible for the overall business process flow.

Workflow B(Secondary Workflow): This workflow handles the approval task. It receives the Approval ID, total number of levels, and current level. If the final approval is granted, it triggers a wait step API  in Workflow A to proceed to the next level. If more approvals are needed, it sends the Approval ID to the next level.

 
Technical Design Steps for Workflow A:
 
  1. Trigger Workflow: The workflow is initiated with input parameters.
  2. Decision Creation: A decision step is created to determine the number of approvals require. 

3. Script Task: This step determines the total number of approvals and the current approval level.

$.context.custom.doneapprovalcount = 0;
$.context.custom.currentcount = 0;
$.context.custom.subwfdefinationid = "Your Secondaray Workflow ID"; // replace with your secondary workflow id
$.context.custom.totalapprovalcount = $.context.decision_getAppprovers_1.Approvers.length;
$.context.custom.triggeredsubprocessids = [];
​
  • Condition Check: If the current level matches the total required approvals, A wait step will be created to wait for the completion. If not, Workflow B is triggered to initiate the approval task.

  • Trigger Workflow B: Workflow B sends the work item to the respective user for approval.
  • Wait Step API : This critical step waits for all parallel approvals to be completed. Once done, the dummy decision is triggered by Workflow B. This is very interesting and latest features
    1. Create a Wait Step with API as follows. After Deployment it will event will create the API End Point which will used to trigger from secondary workflow B .      
    2. Execution ID is the Parent Workflow ID.
    3. Upload the API specification and create the action for this
    4.  Also make sure to create the API key which will be used to trigger it from secondary workflow B.

Technical Design Steps for Workflow B  (Triggered via Workflow A)

  1. Trigger from Workflow A : Workflow B starts when Workflow A reaches a certain point to manage approval tasks separately and in parallel to the main workflow.

  2. Send Approval Task: The approval task is sent to the current level approver to obtain necessary approvals at each level before proceeding.
  3. On Approval: The system retrieves instance details and updates the approval count to keep track of completed approvals and update the workflow status.  
  4. sample code
$.context.custom.approvedcount = $.context.action_get_getV1WorkflowInstancesWorkflowInstanceIdContext_6.result.custom.doneapprovalcount + 1;
  • Check Count: The system checks if all required approvals are obtained:
    • If all approvals are done, the workflow proceeds to the next step.
    • If not all approvals are done This ensures the workflow only proceeds once all approvals are obtained
  •  Update Parent Workflow A Approval Action : increment the update count. created a custom action. If all the approvals not done, then update the approval count via custom action. (refer our previous blog how to create action for workflow API)   
  • Retrieve Task ID & Trigger Wait API trigger wait step ( API) in Workflow A to signal that the approval process is complete / Reject  and it can proceed.
    • Now come to most crucial part of the Development: Trigger the wait step via API
    • Created a custom action project  for the wait step api end point.
    • Here is the Input mapping for the API call. Needs to provide the API Key and Parent ID.
    • It will trigger the wait step in workflow A to approve / move forward the workflow . 
 
In the upcoming blog post - Demonstration with complete end to end testing  , @Adwait_Fadnavis  will delve into the complete end to end testing for these which is very critical to understand the whole development flow.
 
Stay tuned for a comprehensive guide that will enhance your understanding and streamline your Development  process.

Regards,

Mohit Bansal,

SAP Mentor. SAP BTP Solution Architect

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build: Highlights from SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
As the SAP Sapphire 2025 global keynote outlined, this year's focus was clear: AI, applications, and data. SAP Build continues to evolve in alignment with these themes, helping organizations efficiently build, automate, and extend their business processes and applications.

Here's a roundup of Sapphire's key SAP Build announcements and innovations to watch this year.

Joule Studio: Intelligent AI Agents and Skills Builder

Joule Studio Capabilities

SAP unveiled Joule Studio, a new environment within SAP Build that helps you create, deploy, and manage AI agents and custom skills. With Joule Studio, teams can enrich Joule with company-specific skills and intelligent agents that work across SAP and third-party systems. Joule Studio brings two powerful capabilities:

  • Joule Skills: Design modular, reusable skills that can be invoked during conversations. These skills can trigger actions, pull data, and orchestrate SAP Build components like workflows, automations, or apps.
  • AI Agents: Go beyond skills by building full-fledged AI agents that dynamically execute business tasks using reasoning capabilities. These agents use business context, data from SAP systems, and large language models to adapt to user needs.

Joule Studio will be available by the end of Q2 2025, with Agent Builder capabilities expected in Q4 2025.

Early adopters like Sovanta, an SAP partner, already use Joule Studio to enhance customer service. Sovanta significantly streamlined support processes and accelerated ticket resolution by integrating conversational skills with SAP systems.

Another early adopter, Pricefx - a global leader in cloud-based pricing software - is using Joule Studio to bring AI-driven intelligence into their solution. By developing an Agentic AI copilot, they aim to deliver contextual, business-aware guidance that enhances pricing decisions for their customers.

Interested in learning more? Register for the webcast: Meet Joule Studio: Drive Innovation with AI Agents & Joule Skills.

New AI Capabilities for Business Users and Developers

  • SAP Build Work Zone + Joule: Employees can now use Joule to find relevant apps, tasks, and documents faster. It can summarize or generate content based on your business data, saving time and boosting productivity.

Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

  • AI-Enhanced ABAP Development: Developers migrating to SAP Cloud ERP can use AI to convert custom ECC code into ABAP Cloud standards. This feature simplifies modernization efforts while ensuring code quality and compliance.

These capabilities are expected in Q3 2025.

 Expanded SAP Build Capabilities

SAP Build continues to grow as a unified platform with:

  • Extensibility Wizard for SAP Business Suite: A guided wizard that helps you build side-by-side extensions with the proper context, faster. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP SuccessFactors support is planned for release in H2 of 2025.

Extensibility Wizard in SAP Business Suite

Watch Sapphire Customer Session: Extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build at OGE: Open Grid Europe used SAP Build to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud—boosting onboarding efficiency by 50% and reducing invoicing time by 20%.

Watch Sapphire Solution Demo: Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud: SAP Build application development and automation. Discover how to rapidly personalize and extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while maintaining a clean core.

  • SAP Business Data Cloud Integration: Planned for Q4 2025, SAP Build will offer deep integration with SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) via CAP. Developers can consume and expose data products using virtual tables in SAP HANA Cloud, accelerating the development of insight-rich apps while reducing manual integration work. This improves data consistency and supports scalable, compliant development.
  • Event-Driven Applications: SAP Build, in combination with SAP Integration Suite and CAP, will support event-based interactions across systems. General availability is expected in H2 2025.

Simplified Pricing and Packaging

  • SAP Build offers a unified pricing model that combines app development, automation, and workspaces under a single entitlement. This simplifies adoption, reduces licensing complexity, and allows teams to build and extend at their own pace. Learn more in this blog post.

Thanks for reading! Which SAP Build innovation are you most excited about? Let us know - and don't miss these key resources from SAP Sapphire 2025:

  • Watch Sapphire Global Keynote: Your Best. Made Real. No Matter What Lies Ahead
  • Read SAP Sapphire 2025 Innovation Guide
  • Visit the Session Catalog with On-Demand Sessions

entry.title: Accelerate Every Stage of Your SAP Transformation with Enterprise Automation from UiPath and SAP
entry.content:
SAP customers are under pressure to move faster—modernize core systems, accelerate your S/4HANA journey, adopt AI, and do it all while maintaining stability across your operations. That’s why SAP has made enterprise automation a strategic priority across SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

At the center of this strategy is SAP’s Solution Extension partnership with UiPath, the recognized leader in both Robotic Process Automation (RPA) and Intelligent Document Processing (IDP).

By embedding UiPath’s market-leading automation technology directly into SAP Build, SAP delivers a natively integrated solution that empowers you to:

  • Automate across your entire IT landscape, including SAP and non-SAP systems
  • Extend your ERP with automation instead of custom code, maintaining a clean core before, during, and after migration
  • Accelerate your S/4HANA migration by using automation at every phase
  • Include any document in your workflows using enterprise-grade IDP
  • Scale automation securely with built-in governance and native BTP integration

With the UiPath and SAP joint solution, you gain a powerful foundation for end-to-end automation—no matter how complex your systems or processes. From business processes that cut across SAP and non-SAP systems alike—connecting data, decisions, and actions across ERPs, CRMs, emails, documents, and more—UiPath and SAP let you level up to a holistic approach to automation that drives transformational outcomes and AI readiness.

This new eBook from ASUG and UiPath explores how automation helps SAP customers move faster, stay flexible, and prepare for the future with confidence.

In this eBook, you’ll learn:

  • How automation supports every stage of your SAP transformation—from ECC to S/4HANA and beyond
  • How SAP Build and UiPath work together on BTP to reduce complexity, increase agility, and deliver long-term value
  • Why automation is essential for maintaining a clean core, enabling composable architecture, and scaling AI across your business
